title: Bump Buildah to v1.41.0
body:
Bump Buildah to v1.41.0 and adjust as necessary for new commands to the Podman build commands.


<!--
Thanks for sending a pull request!

Please make sure you've read our contributing guidelines and how to submit a pull request (https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/CONTRIBUTING.md#submitting-pull-requests).

Finally, be sure to sign commits with your real name. Since by opening
a PR you already have commits, you can add signatures if needed with
something like `git commit -s --amend`.
-->

#### Does this PR introduce a user-facing change?

<!--
If no, just write `None` in the release-note block below. If yes, a release note
is required: Enter your extended release note in the block below. If the PR
requires additional action from users switching to the new release, include the
string "action required".

For more information on release notes, please follow the Kubernetes model:
https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/release-notes.md
-->

```release-note
None
```

comments.body: I've added the Do Not Merge label on this until we add the CLI changes for the new options too, either here, or in other PRs.

https://api.cirrus-ci.com/v1/artifact/task/6402481032265728/html/bud-podman-fedora-42-root-host-sqlite.log.html

Ok this look about to be expected now as the new tests run and fail now.

Note there also seems to be a new test helper from https://github.com/containers/buildah/pull/6264 that must be compiled now as well for the tests, see https://github.com/containers/podman/commit/c1653bdc29ab1ce76b6127764ab1d9f102518f04 on how to add that one

`podman image diff` tests needs updating too, it seems it now behaves more sanely and actually only showing the three changed files. Checking locally I see the old version always printed `C /etc` so that seems like a legit bug fix in buildah so just update the test to expect three files.
Repushed with @flouthoc's inherit-anotations and unsetannotations updates.  Also added the passwd test helper.
[NON-BLOCKING] Packit jobs failed. @containers/packit-build please check. Everyone else, feel free to ignore.
Added a new commit to fix the `image diff` test issue @luap99 noted.  Holler if that's incorrect.
I've just cherry picked @nalind 's commits in.  TYVM @nalind!

@mheon @Luap99 @nalind I'm now getting this error across many tests:

```
[+0854s]   [FAIL] Podman diff [It] podman diff container and image with same name
[+0854s]   /var/tmp/go/src/github.com/containers/podman/test/e2e/diff_test.go:153

digging:
  [FAILED] Expected
               <[]string | len:1, cap:1>: [
                   "A /8cad9c29088d4aa4ea7c6f1225f0d04d34d04e671b67c91f789fa6412733a126",
               ]
           to have length 2
```

if I do:

```
podman build -t podman-diff-test --layers=false .
podman run --name podman-diff-test podman-diff-test
podman image diff podman-diff-test
```
Using Podman v5.5, I see returned from the diff command

```
C /etc
A /2345
```

But using Podman v5.6 with Buildah 1.41.0 the diff returns:

```
A /2345
```

Both versions of `podman diff` return the same value, and ditto the run command.  I'm pretty sure this is a change in the build command somwhere.   @nalind any thoughts?
That's https://github.com/containers/buildah/pull/6233 working as expected.

OK, I'm officially going nuts.  I adjusted the diff test to check for 1 string, and now it's bringing back 2 and failing.  I'm going to drop the commit for the test that changed it to 1, and will call it a night.
And now it's failing again with getting 1 string when expecting 2.  Off to bed for me.  I'll run through it all from stem to stern in the morning.

you should squash https://github.com/containers/podman/pull/26666/commits/858f10d0b088fae80a1bcd5e8655c1c4ea6aeb72 https://github.com/containers/podman/pull/26666/commits/e740a5bd58dba6c7e17f32e95d16f745f38e3534 as it is the same change then

Though I agree it seems like something is weird here, I think the first thing is they should be all consistent 1 here:
https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/e740a5bd58dba6c7e17f32e95d16f745f38e3534/test/e2e/diff_test.go#L149-L167

looking at the looks there are actually two different failures
```
           $ podman [options] diff podman-diff-test
           A /e14b66c63738f14b2ea0b62676379dff4780a4b0afaf2c690e32860acab1d9c1
           C /etc

           [FAILED] Expected
               <[]string | len:2, cap:2>: [
                   "A /e14b66c63738f14b2ea0b62676379dff4780a4b0afaf2c690e32860acab1d9c1",
                   "C /etc",
               ]
           to have length 1
```
on rawhide and f42 using overlayfs

and
```
           # podman [options] image diff podman-diff-test
           A /37ac6cea9e4aaea217a2b5354fb910b390c30db46400e906c87cbe6fe14033f6

           [FAILED] Expected
               <[]string | len:1, cap:1>: [
                   "A /37ac6cea9e4aaea217a2b5354fb910b390c30db46400e906c87cbe6fe14033f6",
               ]
           to have length 2
```
on f41 using vfs

So I guess the build behavior then depends on the storage driver for that, not sure if that is expected @nalind?
`podman image diff` and `podman diff` are different commands.  I'm thinking maybe you changed the expectation for `podman diff` when you meant to change it for `podman image diff`?

`podman diff` picks the image diff here so it should behave the same as `podman image diff`



On f41(vfs) tests
```
# podman [options] diff podman-diff-test
A /37ac6cea9e4aaea217a2b5354fb910b390c30db46400e906c87cbe6fe14033f6
# podman [options] image diff podman-diff-test
A /37ac6cea9e4aaea217a2b5354fb910b390c30db46400e906c87cbe6fe14033f6
```

On f42 (overlayfs)it fails on the first podman diff since it shows two lines
```
# podman [options] diff podman-diff-test
C /etc
A /faf27587417b6e3708638c6cfab85721180e6789c48918b959fe1ed9c652427a
```

So the issue is that the diff behavior does not seem to be consistent for the storage driver or maybe another variable that is different between f41 and f42
I'm going to put together a commit that removes that check for now.  I just want to see if it gets further down the build path without it.
And @Luap99 nice eyeball on the duplicate commits.  I've squished.

The test previously expected two lines at https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/test/e2e/diff_test.go#L153, and that test now seems to be producing output that's different in different test configurations.  The affected ones are

* https://api.cirrus-ci.com/v1/artifact/task/4653290438262784/html/int-remote-rawhide-root-host-sqlite.log.html#t--Podman-diff-podman-diff-container-and-image-with-same-name--1
* https://api.cirrus-ci.com/v1/artifact/task/4794027926618112/html/int-podman-rawhide-root-host-sqlite.log.html#t--Podman-diff-podman-diff-container-and-image-with-same-name--1
* https://api.cirrus-ci.com/v1/artifact/task/6482877786882048/html/int-remote-fedora-41-root-host-boltdb.log.html#t--Podman-diff-podman-diff-container-and-image-with-same-name--1
* https://api.cirrus-ci.com/v1/artifact/task/6060665321816064/html/int-podman-fedora-41-rootless-host-boltdb.log.html#t--Podman-diff-podman-diff-container-and-image-with-same-name--1
* https://api.cirrus-ci.com/v1/artifact/task/6201402810171392/html/int-podman-fedora-41-root-host-boltdb.log.html#t--Podman-diff-podman-diff-container-and-image-with-same-name--1

That's Raw Hide using overlay, and f41 using vfs.  The /etc directory shows up in the container diff if podman modified it, for example by adding something to /etc that wasn't in the image.  The image includes a /etc/hosts and /etc/hostname, but not a /etc/resolv.conf.  When I run a command using `quay.io/libpod/alpine:latest` and then mount it afterwards, I see an empty /etc/resolv.conf in the rootfs, but `podman diff` doesn't mention it.  I'll keep digging.

It also previously expected, and still expects, two lines at https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/main/test/e2e/diff_test.go#L159, and I expect it to fail there until we change it to expect one line.

Maybe we should just skip the "podman diff container and image with same name" test for now and get this here in so we can start with rc1? I mean clearly this is something worth to investigate and fix but given it seems to be only that rarely podman diff command I doubt it matters for now. We can still have some weeks to fix it for the final.


However I also still see swagger documentation issues from the Test Swagger task
```
- "paths./libpod/build.post.parameters" must validate one and only one schema (oneOf). Found none valid
- paths./libpod/build.post.parameters.in in body should be one of [header]
- paths./libpod/build.post.parameters.type in body should be one of [string number boolean integer array]
- "/libpod/build.POST.parameters.timestamp" must validate one and only one schema (oneOf). Found none valid
- /libpod/build.POST.parameters.timestamp.in in body should be one of [header]
- /libpod/build.POST.parameters.timestamp.type in body should be one of [string number boolean integer array]
```

These should certainly be fixed so we can build a proper API doc.

Yeah, I mistakenly pulled part of the swagger from 'build endpoint: document the "timestamp" flag' into the corresponding 'remote build: relay more new flags introduced in buildah 1.41', but between there and here a "number" was changed back to an "int64".
@nalind do you have a suggested change, or at this point, should I just create a new PR and pull from your repo again?  It might be good to do a clean sweep from scratch on this.
You should be able to rebase --onto my buildah-1.41.0 branch, give or take the most recent changes to the "podman diff container and image with same name" test, which aren't in there.

So, clarifying question: when an image and a container both match a given name, which one is "podman diff" supposed to look at?  I know we had the same question with "inspect" way back, but I neglected to document what the tool did for that case in that man page, too.
Answer: the `podman diff` implementation checks for an image first.
Just redid with the latest from @nalind 's branch.  Crossing multiple fingers and toes.

Ah, the stray /etc comes in when it's built on overlay _and_ "native" diff is used.  The "native" overlay diff walks the upper directory and includes everything it finds in the layer, and that puts the /etc directory in the layer because it was pulled up, even though its timestamp/ownership/permissions aren't changed relative to the lower, and the "upper" directory that it's in has no contents.  We'll have to fix that in buildah.

> Maybe we should just skip the "podman diff container and image with same name" test for now and get this here in so we can start with rc1?

Yeah, you're going to need to do this for rc1, because podman shouldn't have to be checking for the conditions that influence it one way or the other.
Woot!  6 failing tests, and they all look to be flakes for networks or machines not up as expected.  Rerunning.
The Farm tests are failing, should I bother restarting at this point?

tmt is not blocking and doesn't matter so no point in restarting, if I had to guess by looking at the log they got broken by the useradd update which no longer adds subuids.

@containers/podman-maintainers PTAL this should be good to go for the rc1

/lgtm
Thanks all for the help and consults on this beast.  @Luap99 I'll go spin up a PR to Skip rather than comment out those tests.
